basics.name: Enea Manzi
basics.label: Cyber Security Researcher
basics.summary: Cyber Security graduate (M.Sc., University of Milan, 110/110 cum laude), currently applying for an intersectoral Ph.D. programme at the University of Milan. Research focus on API and cloud-native security, with hands-on experience in Kubernetes, Docker and REST APIs.

highlights: Thesis: Design and Implementation of an API-Agnostic Tool for REST API Security Assurance
Supervisors: Marco Anisetti, Claudio Agostino Ardagna
Final Grade: 110/110 cum laude
Thesis: Design and Implementation of a Framework for Evaluating Distributed Systems
Supervisors: Marco Anisetti, Filippo Berto
Final Grade: 110/110 cum laude
Final Grade: 93/100

work.name: SESAR Lab, University of Milan
work.summary: Secure Service-oriented Architectures Research Lab (SESAR Lab)
highlights: Designed and implemented **APIGuard Assurance**, an **API-agnostic**, **contract-driven** tool for automated REST API **security assurance** based on a taxonomy of eight domains and *specified oracles* to establish **trust** in automated verdicts, validated against a real-world target.

work.name: SESAR Lab, University of Milan
work.summary: Secure Service-oriented Architectures Research Lab (SESAR Lab)
highlights: Contributed to the design and implementation of an **Assurance Engine** for the verification of **non-functional properties (NFP)** in distributed systems, built in **Rust** and deployed on **Kubernetes**, developing a new connector to integrate **Elasticsearch** as an **observability** backend.

work.name: DaTrik Solutions
highlights: Developed a web application using HTML, JavaScript, and jQuery as part of a high school work-based learning program (PCTO).

research.name: SESAR Lab, University of Milan
research.summary: Secure Service-oriented Architectures Research Lab (SESAR Lab)
highlights: Analyzed existing approaches to REST API testing, observing that most focus on **functional** and **syntactic** conformance, while **security violations** remain comparatively unaddressed.
Defined a taxonomy of **eight security domains** to structure systematic assessment coverage.

Proposed an **API-agnostic**, **contract-driven** methodology, based on *specified oracles*, that derives target knowledge exclusively from the **OpenAPI Specification (OAS)** and a deployment configuration file.

Addressed the *oracle problem* in automated security testing, central to establishing **trust** in an automated verdict, by deriving, for each security control, an evaluation criterion from the specific knowledge and expectations of the test being performed, traceable to **OWASP** categories and **CWE** codes.

Achieved **deterministic**, **reproducible** results, a prerequisite for assurance integrated into continuous development, through a **DAG**-based scheduling approach exposing **semantic exit codes** for **CI/CD** integration.

Validated the methodology against a real-world target, **Forgejo** exposed through the **Kong** API gateway, confirming correct detection of security violations and deterministic, reproducible results.

research.name: SESAR Lab, University of Milan
research.summary: Secure Service-oriented Architectures Research Lab (SESAR Lab)
highlights: Investigated existing approaches to verifying and monitoring **non-functional properties (NFP)** in distributed **Edge-Cloud Continuum** systems.
Contributed to the development of an **Assurance Engine** for distributed systems, building a new connector to integrate **Elasticsearch** as an **observability** backend.

Formalized contracts to jointly evaluate multiple non-functional properties (**CIA triad**, reliability, scalability, performance), validated experimentally on a **Kubernetes** testbed with minimal computational overhead.

projects.name: APIGuard Assurance
projects.summary:
Master's Thesis Project (A.Y. 2025/2026)

Development of an API-agnostic, contract-driven tool for REST API security assurance, with a particular focus on semantic security violations that traditional functional and syntactic testing does not capture.
highlights: Built in **Python 3.12**, with a **dynamic test-discovery mechanism** allowing new security checks to be added without modifying the core engine.
Implemented *specified oracles* directly in each test's code: every check encodes its own evaluation criterion, derived from the domain knowledge of the control under test.
Enforced **strict static typing** (mypy) and **runtime validation** (Pydantic), with full documentation coverage across the public API.
Built **reproducible packages** with **Hatch**, verifying identical SHA-256 hashes across repeated builds.
Designed an **extensible** connector architecture to integrate external security scanners, decoupling test logic from tool-specific output parsing.

projects.name: Event-Driven IoT Architecture (Kafka + Kong)
projects.summary:
Cloud Computing Technologies Course Project (A.Y. 2025/2026)

Development of a cloud-native microservices infrastructure on Kubernetes for IoT ingestion and monitoring, with a particular focus on **non-functional properties** such as scalability, fault tolerance, and edge-to-core security.
highlights: Microservices architecture based on **Apache Kafka** in KRaft mode (ZooKeeper-less) for asynchronous decoupling and reliable message handling (*Zero Data Loss*).
Integration of **Kong API Gateway** to implement the *Gateway Offloading* pattern, centralizing Authentication (API Key), Rate Limiting, and Load Balancing at the edge.
Storage layer optimized on **MongoDB** using **Time Series Collections** and hybrid compression (LZ4/Zstd) to maximize storage efficiency and analytical performance.
Full **Kubernetes** orchestration managed via **Helm**, featuring automatic scalability (HPA) and advanced security (Secrets, TLS/SASL) to meet non-functional requirements.

projects.name: FTP Client and Server
projects.summary:
Computer Networks Course Project (A.Y. 2022-2023)

Developed a multi-threaded **FTP-compliant** client-server application in **C** for file transfer, fully interoperable with **FileZilla**.
highlights: Architecture: Multi-threaded server based on **Socket Programming** for concurrent session management.
Data Modes: Native support for both Active (PORT) and Passive (PASV) data transfer modes.
Protocol Management: Implemented essential *FTP commands* (e.g., USER, PASS, LIST, RETR, STOR, CWD...)
**Key skills:** **C**, **socket programming**, **multithreading**, **network protocols**, **FTP**, **Git**.

projects.name: Exhibition Web App
projects.summary:
Web and Mobile Programming Course Project (A.Y. 2021-2022)

Built a dynamic web application serving as a digital portfolio for displaying multimedia content.
highlights: Developed the backend with *Node.js/Express* and the frontend with *HTML5*, *CSS3*, *JavaScript/jQuery*.
Integrated *MongoDB* for document-oriented storage and *AJAX* for asynchronous client-server communication (GET, POST, DELETE).
Managed source code and collaboration using **Git** and **GitHub**.
**Key skills:** *Node.js*, *Express*, *MongoDB*, *AJAX*, *REST APIs*, *jQuery*, **Git**.

publications.title: Security Assurance for Modern Big Data APIs
publications.summary:
**Enea Manzi, Marco Anisetti**

Automated REST API testing has traditionally focused on functional compliance, leaving security assurance comparatively underexplored; existing tools operate at the syntactic level and cannot reliably detect the semantic vulnerabilities behind several top-ranked OWASP API Security categories. This work proposes a contract-driven approach that derives all target knowledge from the OpenAPI specification and a configuration file, embedding evaluation criteria directly into deterministic test oracles and organizing checks into an eight-domain taxonomy scaling with available privileges. Tested on a realistic setup, the approach correctly classified expected outcomes, revealed a real discrepancy between declared and actual API behavior, and produced reproducible results, contributing a systematic approach to the oracle problem and a process ready for integration into automated pipelines.
